race.name: Viru Maraton
race.tagline: Forty-two kilometers through the ancient forests of Virumaa — Estonia's classic-technique marathon where Baltic pine and frozen bog define an Estoloppet staple.

climate.description: February in Estonia's Virumaa brings cold continental conditions tempered by Baltic maritime influence. Temperatures typically -10°C to -2°C. Snow reliability is moderate — the Baltic climate can produce mild spells, though the inland Virumaa location is more reliable than the coast.
challenges: Baltic thaw risk — maritime air masses can bring above-freezing temperatures
Flat terrain demands sustained classic technique over 42km
Snow conditions can vary from powder to crust
Low altitude provides no elevation-based snow guarantee

course.primary: Classic-technique course through the forests and bogland of Virumaa, northeastern Estonia. Flat to gently undulating terrain typical of the Baltic. Pine forest and frozen bog provide the landscape. Well-groomed set tracks for classic technique.
course.surface: Machine-groomed classic tracks through Estonian forest
features: Classic technique only — pure diagonal stride and double pole
Part of the Estoloppet series — Estonia's national ski marathon circuit
Virumaa's ancient forests and raised bogs
Flat Baltic terrain tests endurance over technique
Estonian forest landscape with pine, spruce, and birch
Good domestic field as part of the Estoloppet calendar

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Solid Estoloppet series event with classic-only format. Flat Baltic terrain and low altitude limit multiple scores. Snow reliability is a concern in the Baltic climate. Good domestic community engagement as part of Estonia's national series.

history.summary: The Viru Maraton is part of the Estoloppet series, Estonia's national circuit of long-distance ski marathons. Held in the Virumaa region of northeastern Estonia, the race traverses the ancient forests and raised bogs that characterize the Baltic landscape. Classic technique only, honoring the traditional style of Nordic skiing.
notable_facts: Part of the Estoloppet series — Estonia's national ski marathon circuit
Classic technique only — one of few marathon-distance classic-only events in the Baltics
Virumaa is one of Estonia's most forested regions
Estonia's raised bogs are unique European ecosystems
Estonian skiing tradition dates to the medieval period
The Estoloppet series connects ski communities across Estonia
